steps.title: Find the constants
steps.content:
From $(0,-\sqrt{326})$, substitute $x=0$:
$$
-\sqrt{c}=-\sqrt{326}
$$
so $c=326$. From $(2,0)$, the radicand must be $0$:
$$
4+2b+326=0
$$
so $b=-165$.

steps.title: Set the radicand to zero
steps.content:
If $h(m)=0$, then
$$
m^2-165m+326=0
$$
Use the quadratic formula:
$$
m=\frac{165\pm \sqrt{165^2-4(326)}}{2}=\frac{165\pm 161}{2}
$$

steps.title: Choose the greater value
steps.content: The two possible values are $$ \frac{165+161}{2}=163,\quad \frac{165-161}{2}=2 $$ The greater value comes from the plus sign. The answer is 163.
